feature_list.test_description: Verify direct support for 100 Kbps normal-mode I2C operation.
feature_list.test_constraints: Where implemented; only 100 Kbps and 400 Kbps modes are supported directly.
test_steps: Configure the core for normal-mode I2C operation at 100 Kbps.
Apply I2C traffic using SCL and SDA at the configured rate.
expected_results: The core operates at 100 Kbps normal mode.
feature_list.requirement_description: The core can directly support 100 Kbps normal-mode I2C operation, where implemented.
feature_list.requirement_text:
The interface defines 3 transmission speeds:
- Normal: 100Kbps
Only 100Kbps and 400Kbps modes are supported directly.

feature_list.test_description: Verify direct support for 400 Kbps fast-mode I2C operation.
feature_list.test_constraints: Where correct hardware is included; only 100 Kbps and 400 Kbps modes are supported directly.
test_steps: Configure the core for fast-mode I2C operation at 400 Kbps.
Apply I2C traffic using SCL and SDA at the configured rate.
expected_results: The core operates at 400 Kbps fast mode.
feature_list.requirement_description: The core can support 400 Kbps fast-mode I2C operation, where correct hardware is included.
feature_list.requirement_text:
The interface defines 3 transmission speeds:
- Fast: 400Kbps
Only 100Kbps and 400Kbps modes are supported directly.

feature_list.test_description: Verify the core presents a WISHBONE RevB.3 compliant WISHBONE Classic interface.
test_steps: Connect the core to a WISHBONE RevB.3 Classic bus environment.
Perform standard WISHBONE accesses using the listed interface signals.
expected_results: The interface operates as a WISHBONE RevB.3 Classic interface.
Standard WISHBONE bus transactions are accepted using the defined signals.
feature_list.requirement_description: The core shall implement a WISHBONE RevB.3 compliant WISHBONE Classic interface.
feature_list.requirement_text: The core features a WISHBONE RevB.3 compliant WISHBONE Classic interface.

feature_list.test_description: Verify each WISHBONE access completes in two clock cycles.
test_steps: Initiate a WISHBONE access on wb_clk_i with wb_cyc_i and wb_stb_i asserted.
Measure the number of clock cycles until wb_ack_o is asserted.
expected_results: wb_ack_o is asserted after two clock cycles for the access.
Each access completes in two clock cycles.
feature_list.requirement_description: Each WISHBONE access must take two clock cycles.

feature_list.test_description: Verify asynchronous reset operates as active high when ARST_LVL is configured to 1'b1.
test_steps: Assert arst_i and observe the core reset behavior.
expected_results: The asynchronous reset is active when arst_i is asserted high.
The core responds to reset in the active-high configuration.
feature_list.requirement_description: The asynchronous reset level is configurable as active high or active low.
feature_list.requirement_text: The asynchronous reset level can be set to either active high (1’b1) or active low (1’b0)

feature_list.test_description: Verify asynchronous reset operates as active low when ARST_LVL is configured to 1'b0.
test_steps: Assert arst_i low and observe the core reset behavior.
expected_results: The asynchronous reset is active when arst_i is asserted low.
The core responds to reset in the active-low configuration.
feature_list.requirement_description: The asynchronous reset level is configurable as active high or active low.
feature_list.requirement_text: The asynchronous reset level can be set to either active high (1’b1) or active low (1’b0)

feature_list.test_description: Verify the core exposes the SCL pad input, output, and output-enable signals.
test_steps: Inspect the external port list for scl_pad_i, scl_pad_o, and scl_pad_oe.
Connect these signals to an external open-drain bus model.
expected_results: scl_pad_i, scl_pad_o, and scl_pad_oe are present as defined signals.
The SCL pad connection supports external open-drain bus interfacing.
feature_list.requirement_description: The core must expose SCL and SDA pad input, output, and output-enable signals for external open-drain bus connection.
feature_list.requirement_text:
scl_pad_i | 1 | Input | Serial Clock line input
scl_pad_o | 1 | Output | Serial Clock line output
scl_pad_oe | 1 | Output | Serial Clock line output enable
sda_pad_i | 1 | Input | Serial Data line input
sda_pad_o | 1 | Output | Serial Data line output
sda_pad_oe | 1 | Output | Serial Data line output enable

feature_list.test_description: Verify the core exposes the SDA pad input, output, and output-enable signals.
test_steps: Inspect the external port list for sda_pad_i, sda_pad_o, and sda_pad_oe.
Connect these signals to an external open-drain bus model.
expected_results: sda_pad_i, sda_pad_o, and sda_pad_oe are present as defined signals.
The SDA pad connection supports external open-drain bus interfacing.
feature_list.requirement_description: The core must expose SCL and SDA pad input, output, and output-enable signals for external open-drain bus connection.
feature_list.requirement_text:
scl_pad_i | 1 | Input | Serial Clock line input
scl_pad_o | 1 | Output | Serial Clock line output
scl_pad_oe | 1 | Output | Serial Clock line output enable
sda_pad_i | 1 | Input | Serial Data line input
sda_pad_o | 1 | Output | Serial Data line output
sda_pad_oe | 1 | Output | Serial Data line output enable
